title: [release-1.16] Add(): fix handling of relative paths with no ContextDir
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

`Add()` is supposed to handle relative paths when the `ContextDir` value passed to it is not set, but it hasn't been doing that correctly since it was overhauled.  Correct it to recognize relative paths at the function start, when `ContextDir` is not set, and combining them with the current directory to convert them to absolute paths.

#### How to verify it

Added an integration test which should cover the reported cases.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #2619.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Cherry picked from #2630.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
The regression in `buildah add` which caused it to not properly handle source locations specified using relative paths should be fixed.
```

comments.body: Tests are failing @nalind I think it's unrelated, but it's an odd error.

@nalind and/or @mheon 
Is this a change we should spin up a new release for and work on getting into Podman sooner rather than later?  Is this exception worthy @nalind in your thinking?  It's close to me.

I think it's worth tagging a 1.16.2 for the sake of our CLI.  A quick grep for "buildah.Add" doesn't turn up any calls in the podman source tree to `buildah.Add()` except from our imagebuildah package, which always supplies a `ContextDir` value, so I don't _think_ podman is affected.
